title: Aspirin Rectal: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text: tal, tell your doctor or pharmacist if you are allergic to this medication, any part of this medication, or any other medications, foods or substances. Tell your doctor or pharmacist about the allergy and what symptoms you had. tell your doctor and pharmacist what prescription and nonprescription medications, vitamins, nutritional supplements, and herbal products you are taking or plan to take while using aspirin rectal. Your doctor may need to change the doses of your medications or monitor you carefully for side effects. the following nonprescription or herbal products may interact with aspirin: nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) such as naproxen or ibuprofen. Be sure to let your doctor and pharmacist know that you are taking these medications before you start using aspirin rectal. Do not start any of these medications while using aspirin rectal without discussing with your healthcare provider. tell your doctor if you have or have ever had asthma, frequent stuffed or runny nose, or nasal polyps (growths on the linings of the nose). If you have these conditions, there is a risk that you will have an allergic reaction to aspirin. Your doctor may tell you that you should not take aspirin. tell your doctor if you have or have ever had kidney or liver disease. Also, tell your doctor if you drink three or more alcoholic drinks every day. ask a doctor before you give aspi
